Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

The sanity-quota failure should be fixed with recent landings. So, the patch can be rebased to get that fix.

The problem is that the conf-sanity test 50h hang is only seen for this patch. Could the lod_object changes becausing the conf-sanity test 50h hang?

Patch Set 2:

It definitely seems possible that the conf-sanity test_50h failure is related to this patch.  The test sets the default stripe count to -1, and then creates a file after reactivating an OST.  This causes the MDS to fail an assertion:

 LustreError: 15385:0:(lod_qos.c:858:lod_comp_ost_in_use()) 
      ASSERTION(inuse->op_count * sizeof(inuse->op_array[0]) < inuse->op_size ) failed: 
 LustreError: 15385:0:(lod_qos.c:858:lod_comp_ost_in_use()) LBUG
 Pid: 15385, comm: mdt00_000

commit.subject: LU-10462 lod: Correct lfs --component-add striping
commit.message:
LU-10462 lod: Correct lfs --component-add striping

lfs --component-add -E <end> -c -1 would apply the default
stripe count to a file when it should be striping across
all OSTs. This was due to incorrect handling of sentinel
values in various portions of lod_object.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Di Natale <dinatale2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: I178f13be81ee546f44edcdadcf7086a1a57f7e9a
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30790
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam@hotmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
